# prd-to-prod

> Send a PRD, get a deployed app. Open-source autonomous pipeline powered by
> GitHub Agentic Workflows. MIT licensed.

## What this is

An autonomous software delivery pipeline. You write a product spec as a GitHub
issue. AI agents decompose it into tasks, implement each one, review the code,
merge approved PRs, deploy to production, and self-heal from CI failures.
Humans own policy, secrets, and escalation decisions.

## Quick start

Fork this repo, install gh-aw (`gh extension install github/gh-aw`), run
`bash scripts/bootstrap.sh && gh aw secrets bootstrap && git push`. Create an
issue with a product spec and comment `/decompose`.

You'll need: GitHub Actions, an LLM license (Copilot, Claude, Codex, or
Gemini), and hosting (Vercel free tier works).

## Core artifacts

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — coding standards, PR rules, repository conventions
- [autonomy-policy.yml](autonomy-policy.yml) — machine-readable authority boundary
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) — how workflows and agents connect

## Pipeline agents

- `prd-decomposer` — splits a spec into ordered sub-issues
- `prd-planner` — creates an architecture plan before coding starts
- `repo-assist` — implements issues and opens PRs
- `frontend-agent` — handles visual/UI issues with design-aware review
- `pr-review-agent` — reviews diffs against the spec and policy
- `ci-doctor` — diagnoses failed CI runs

## Workflow groups

- Entry points and routing — `auto-dispatch`, `auto-dispatch-requeue`, `prd-decomposer`
- Agent workflows — `repo-assist`, `frontend-agent`, `pr-review-agent`, `pr-review-submit`
- Delivery and recovery — CI, deploy-router, failure routing, watchdog, issue cleanup
- Auxiliary — status reporting, code simplification, duplication detection, security compliance

## What humans own

- Workflow files (`.github/workflows/`)
- Policy changes (`autonomy-policy.yml`)
- Secrets and tokens
- Deploy configuration
- Branch protection and merge-scope expansion

## Pricing

The code is free (MIT). Managed service: $99–$499 per pipeline run, all
compute included. Self-hosted: bring your own LLM (~$19/mo) + hosting (~$0–20/mo).
Optional support at $299/mo.
